title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot meets with Superintendent Spence on a glass-enclosed terrace in Woodleigh Village. Spence explains that he has moved in with his sister, Elsa, following the death of her husband and the departure of her children to live abroad. While sharing spiced ginger beer, the two men discuss the recent murder of Joyce, specifically the detail of her head being found in a water bucket.

Poirot attempts to enlist Spence's help, arguing that despite Spence's retirement from the police, he still possesses a detective's perspective and access to local rumors. Spence expresses skepticism, noting that modern social dynamics and the tendency of women to defend "bad boy" partners make investigations difficult.

The conversation shifts to their shared history. Poirot reveals that he was asked to investigate the current case by the mystery novelist Ariadne Oliver. This prompts a recollection of a past case involving the murder of Miss Macquet, which Spence had previously asked Poirot to solve. They briefly reminisce about a difficult individual from that old case, described as a man suited to being a mortician.

Poirot clarifies that Mrs. Oliver was staying at the house of Mrs. Butler, a widow whose husband was a pilot. He explains that Mrs. Oliver traveled to London to find him because she was deeply uneasy about the incident and wanted him to intervene. Although Spence acknowledges that he also once sought Poirot's help, he insists that he cannot assist with the current investigation.
